assertions: The operator-visible text matches exactly: Spec System is not initialised. Run `/z-spec-init` first to create `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`.
`.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is still absent afterward and was not synthesized as a fabricated default layout.
No new `assessment-repo-<YYYYMMDD>.md` under `{specsDir}` and no new `assessment-<feature>-<YYYYMMDD>.md` beside a tracked spec catalogue appear from this halted pass.
cases.expected_output: Palette handling stops cold with only the mandated initialisation message without generating assessment Markdown files.

assertions: Fresh Markdown exists under `{specsDir}` named `assessment-repo-<YYYYMMDD>.md` aligned with documented repository-wide artefact conventions.
The report inspects codebase wiring (agents, skills, commands, hooks, scripts, documentation, conventions) reflecting the enumerated repository-wide completeness pass.
Pros sections address completeness, feasibility, structure, specificity, risk awareness, and convention compliance headings before yielding a summed judgement.

The verdict label reads Approve, Conditional, or Reject keyed to averages at or above 4.0, between 3.0 and 3.9 inclusive, or below 3.0 respectively, matching the mandated rubric cut-points suited to signalling readiness versus rework.
cases.expected_output: A solitary dated repository health critique lands under `{specsDir}`, walks every advertised review axis with concrete repository references, then closes using the sanctioned score tiers.

assertions: After issuing the slash command targeting that directory shard, Markdown appears inside `specs/20260526-batch-export/` using the `assessment-<feature>-<yyyymmdd>.md` naming style tied to catalogue identity.

Narrative cross-checks spec assumptions against on-disk integrations, critiques dependency coherence, scrutinises enumerated subtasks, and highlights materially risky gaps instead of skim-reading only the preamble.
The closing verdict honours Approve, Conditional, or Reject wording with thresholds matching readiness guidance so operators know whether to queue `/z-spec-execute`.
